name: Vega-Lite (Grammar of Interactive Graphics)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 3-Clause); UW Interactive Data Lab; Jeffrey Heer

performance.notes: SVG or Canvas rendering; Vega runtime underneath; larger datasets may need aggregation

agent_readiness.notes: High-level Grammar of Interactive Graphics JSON specification. Concise JSON specs create bar, line, scatter, heatmap, geographic, layered, and faceted charts. Selection-based interactions (hover, click, brush, zoom). Automatic axis, legend, and tooltip generation. Python Altair is a Python API for Vega-Lite. Powers Observable Plot, and many BI tools. Declarative spec → visualization. LLM-friendly: JSON spec is easy to generate programmatically.

gotchas: Large datasets need aggregation before rendering (no GPU backend)
Complex interactions require Vega (not just Vega-Lite)
Debugging invalid specs can be cryptic
Real customization requires dropping to Vega layer
best_when: Declarative data visualization specs for reproducible charts
Interactive charts with selection and linking
BI tools and notebook environments (via Altair in Python)
avoid_when: Very large datasets (> 100k rows) needing WebGL
summary: Vega-Lite is a declarative JSON grammar for interactive data visualizations. Concise specifications create bar, line, scatter, heatmap, and geographic charts with automatic axes, legends, tooltips. Selection-based interactions (brush, hover, click). Python Altair is the Python API. LLM-friendly: JSON specs are easy to generate programmatically. Powers Observable and many BI tools. Standard for academic reproducible visualizations.
